name: Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio
name_original: Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Samartín Del Rei Aurelio are situated in the heart of Asturias, Spain, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de San Mamés and parts of the Cantabrian Mountains, providing challenging ascents and descents. The Nalón River Valley flows through the municipality, contributing varied topography from riverbanks to higher elevations. A significant portion of the area is part of the Protected Landscape of the Mining Basins, ensuring a preserved natural environment for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Samartín Del Rei Aurelio The most popular road cycling route is Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio, a 42.1 miles (67.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de las Señales – Tarna Pass loop from Blimea, a difficult 67.3 miles (108.4 km) path. This route offers extensive climbing and scenic views across the Cantabrian Mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Playa de La Coaña loop from San Martín del Rey Aurelio, a 17.3 miles (27.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Road cycling around Samartín Del Rei Aurelio is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and protected landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain passes. The routes in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 49 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Samartín Del Rei Aurelio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio, offering diverse options for cyclists. These include routes ranging from easy loops to challenging mountain passes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio?
A: Road cycling in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio is characterized by mountainous terrain, including parts of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Sierra de San Mamés. You'll find challenging ascents and descents, as well as varied topography along the Nalón River Valley, from riverbanks to higher elevations.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078379637/alto-de-la-colladiella-y-alto-de-la-colladona-circular-desde-sotrondio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, features significant elevation gain through mountain passes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20163819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Señales – Tarna Pass loop from Blimea&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive climbing and scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling?
A: You can encounter various natural features and landmarks. The region is part of the Protected Landscape of the Mining Basins and is close to the Redes Natural Park. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Praeres – Sierra de Peñamayor&lt;/a&gt; summit, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Bello Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic mountain views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078379637/alto-de-la-colladiella-y-alto-de-la-colladona-circular-desde-sotrondio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de La Coaña loop from San Martín del Rey Aurelio&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain.

Q: Are there easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for less experienced cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40362969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gargantadá – Carbayín Alto loop from El Entrego - La Oscura&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route, offering a more gentle introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio?
A: The road cycling routes in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 49 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, challenging climbs, and the preserved natural environment.

Q: Can I find routes that start from specific towns like Blimea or Sotrondio?
A: Yes, several routes conveniently start from local towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078379637/alto-de-la-colladiella-y-alto-de-la-colladona-circular-desde-sotrondio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Colladiella y Alto de La Colladona – circular desde Sotrondio&lt;/a&gt; begins in Sotrondio, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20163819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Señales – Tarna Pass loop from Blimea&lt;/a&gt; starts from Blimea.

Q: What about parking for cyclists in Samartín Del Rei Aurelio?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, the recreational areas such as La Isla, La Colladiella, Polaúra, La Camperona, and Llantero often provide facilities for visitors, which may include parking suitable for accessing nearby routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists with bikes?
A: Public transport options, including bus and train services, connect various towns within Samartín Del Rei Aurelio and the broader Asturias region. Policies regarding bicycles on public transport can vary, so it's recommended to check with local operators like Renfe Cercanías or regional bus services for their specific bicycle policies before planning your journey.

Q: Are there any protected natural areas I can cycle through?
A: A significant portion of the area is part of the Protected Landscape of the Mining Basins, ensuring the preservation of its natural beauty. The nearby Redes Natural Park, a Biosphere Reserve, also contributes to the rich natural environment, characterized by mountains, rivers, and forests, offering scenic routes through protected landscapes.

Q: Are there recreational areas along the routes for rest?
A: Yes, the municipality provides several recreational areas such as La Isla, La Colladiella, Polaúra, La Camperona, and Llantero. These areas offer places for rest and leisure amidst the natural surroundings, often featuring viewpoints with panoramic views of the mountains and protected landscapes.
tips: The top of La Colladiella is a pass with very little traffic and a good firm ground. On these lost roads you can enjoy cycling pleasantly, despite its steep slopes.

Driving along the road from Ujo to Puerto de San Isidro we have a magnificent view of the Soto castle, built in the 12th century, and rebuilt during the 14th and 15th centuries. The construction is in a dilapidated state, but it is worth seeing this marvel declared an Asset of Cultural Interest.
Great route for the road bike, in which you can do 100 kilometers nailed, making a circular route from Oviedo, passing through Gargantada on the way.

waypoints.name: View of Soto de Aller Castle
